# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Testing for Community Contributions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
        with:
          submodules: true
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Python and Install Dependencies
        run: |
          python --version
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run Tests
        env:
          TEST_RESULT: ''
        run: |
          if $(testinfra test); then
            TEST_RESULT='success'
          else
            TEST_RESULT='failure'
          fi

      - name: Post Test Results as Comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            if [ "$TEST_RESULT" = "success" ]; then
              gh comment issue --repo=your-repo-name --issue-number=${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME} 'Tests ran successfully'
            else
              gh comment issue --repo=your-repo-name --issue-number=${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME} "Tests failed: \n$(testinfra output)"
            fi
